summary: Task 03 introduces no new attack surface. The only untrusted input is the sysinfo.* JSON blob read from a user-supplied tar archive; it is decoded with json.Unmarshal into a fixed struct (stat.SysInfo{Ticks float64, CPUCount int}) — Go's encoding/json does not provide deserialization gadgets, and the struct has no methods. Read size is double-bounded (tar.Reader internally limits to hdr.Size; the report code additionally wraps the reader in io.LimitReader(r, hdr.Size) before io.ReadAll). Sysinfo entries in real recordings are tiny (~30 bytes). No new file system writes, no shell execution, no new env-var reads, no new network surface. The -N CLI flag is a bool; no injection vector. The added metadata fields (ticks, cpuCount) are not used in arithmetic or path construction in this task — they are stored for future use under Option B.

owaspCoverage.A03_Injection: no_findings — fixed JSON struct decode; no SQL/shell/path concatenation
owaspCoverage.A04_Insecure_Design: no_findings — design follows existing pgcenter tar/JSON pattern
owaspCoverage.A08_Software_Data_Integrity: no_findings — JSON decode errors are wrapped and surfaced; archive corruption returns error rather than silently miscounting
owaspCoverage.A09_Security_Logging_Monitoring: no_findings — INFO/WARNING lines are user-facing report output, not security telemetry

specificChecks.detail: io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r, hdr.Size)) caps allocation at the tar header's declared size; tar.Reader independently enforces the same limit. A malicious tar declaring a multi-GB sysinfo size would still fail at tar layer first.

stat.SysInfo has only exported scalar fields (float64, int). json.Unmarshal into this shape cannot trigger code execution, panic on malformed input is converted to error and surfaced. Unknown JSON fields are silently ignored — acceptable for forward-compat.
Wrapped errors include the tar entry name (controlled by recorder, not user-injected for a typical workflow). No file paths, secrets, or internal state leaked.

readTar still gates data channel send on metaOK && statOK as before. Sysinfo branch does not block on channel sends. The new processData no-data INFO path executes inside the existing doneCh case — no new select branches or channels.
WARNING/INFO strings are constant literals written to app.writer (stdout in production). No format-string injection (fmt.Fprint with constant string).
